name: Buttermilk Honey Bread
recipeIngredient: 1 envelope dry yeast or 1 cake fresh yeast
5 3/4 to 6 1/4 cups all-purpose flour or bread flour
2 tablespoons (1/4 stick) unsalted butter, melted and cooled
recipeInstructions:
Makes 2 oval loaves
1. In a small bowl, sprinkle dry yeast over warm water (105-115°F). Add 1 teaspoon honey and stir to dissolve. If using cake yeast, crumble yeast into the small bowl. Stir in lukewarm water (95°F) and 1 teaspoon honey. Let stand until foamy, about 10 minutes.
2. In a large bowl, combine 2 cups flour, buttermilk, 3 tablespoons honey, butter, and salt. Add yeast mixture and whisk until smooth, about 3 minutes. Using a wooden spoon, mix in enough remaining flour (about 1/2 cup at a time) until the dough is too stiff to stir.
3. Knead the dough on a floured surface until it is smooth and satiny, kneading in more flour if sticky, about 10 minutes.
4. Grease a large bowl. Add dough, turning to coat the entire surface. Cover the bowl with plastic wrap. Let rise in a warm, draft-free area until doubled, about 1 1/2 hours.
5. Grease 2 baking sheets. Add dough, turning to coat lightly. Let rest on a lightly floured surface until deflated, about 10 minutes. Cut dough into half. Knead each piece into a round. Gently pull the opposite sides together to form an oval. Place on prepared sheets, seam side down. Cover with a towel. Let rise in a warm, draft-free area until doubled, about 45 minutes.
6. Position rack in center of oven and preheat to 375°F. Bake until brown and loaves sound hollow when tapped on the bottom, about 45 minutes. Immediately transfer to racks. Cool completely before slicing.
